fields.description:
## Context
The identity epic shipped the merge-gate as `.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml`, which walks
the MERGED LOG and grandfathers via `ENFORCE_SINCE` (CLI `rebar verify-identity`,
`src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py`). This story adds a **required-environment op-cert
verification lane** that reuses that merged-log walk + grandfathering rather than standing up a
parallel walker.

**Op-cert storage shape (defined here; consumed by ee0b producer + 8f1d migration).** An op-cert
is stored on a ticket in the `attestations` map under its kind (`plan-review` /
`completion-verifier`) as a record carrying a **DSSE `envelope`** (`{payload, payloadType,
signatures:[{keyid, sig}]}`) — the asymmetric variant of today's HMAC record — plus the bound
`{ticket_id, material_fingerprint, merged_log_commit}` fields needed to call
`trusted_env.verify_required_environment`. This story only READS that shape (a fixture synthesizes
one); ee0b's trusted server and 8f1d's migration PRODUCE it.

**Scope = verify-side + grandfathered.** Because producers (ee0b/8f1d) land later, the lane is
**advisory until `ENFORCE_SINCE`** covers op-cert-bearing commits — exactly the identity gate's
transition mechanism — so adding it does not flip the repo to blocking before op-certs exist.

**Grandfathering the ABSENCE case (the missing-op-cert enforcement anchor).** The identity gate's
`_is_enforced(commit_sha, since_ref)` tests the commit that introduced *an event*. A ticket that
carries NO op-cert has no such event/commit. Resolution: the enforcement anchor for a ticket
lacking a required-environment op-cert is the commit that introduced the ticket's **anchoring gate
event** — the ticket's `CLOSE` event for the completion-verifier lane (the event an op-cert would
attest) — resolved with the same `authorship.resolve_event_commit` mechanism the identity gate
uses. That anchor commit is fed to `_is_enforced`: a ticket whose close event predates
`ENFORCE_SINCE` is grandfathered (advisory pass); one after it is enforced, so a missing/foreign
op-cert fails. A ticket with no anchoring event yet (not closed) is out of scope for the
completion-verifier lane.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A CLI entrypoint `rebar verify-opcert` (in `src/rebar/_commands/`, mirroring
      `verify_authorship.py`) accepts `--require-environment <env_id>` (default from
      `verify.require_environment` config) and `--since <ref>` (grandfathering), walks the merged
      log's tickets (reusing the merged-log walk helper `verify_authorship.py` uses), and for each
      in-scope ticket verifies its required-kind op-cert via
      `rebar.attest.trusted_env.verify_required_environment` against `.rebar/trusted_environments.yaml`.
      Exit 1 iff an ENFORCED in-scope ticket lacks a valid required-environment op-cert; else exit 0
      (advisory). A unit test asserts the exit-code contract.
- [ ] `.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml` gains an op-cert verification **step** (same job /
      same merged-log walk + `ENFORCE_SINCE`, NOT a parallel job) invoking `rebar verify-opcert`.
      A test/assertion confirms the workflow file references `verify-opcert` and reuses the shared
      `ENFORCE_SINCE` var.
- [ ] A fixture test proves the gate: a ticket carrying a valid op-cert (DSSE envelope signed by a
      test env key pinned in a fixture `.rebar/trusted_environments.yaml`) PASSES; a ticket
      missing the op-cert, and a ticket whose op-cert is signed by a foreign (non-pinned) key,
      both FAIL. Fixture location: `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/` (integration) or
      `tests/unit/test_verify_opcert.py`; the test names the command it runs.
- [ ] CI-trigger audit as a concrete artifact: a comment block in
      `.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml` enumerates its `on:` triggers and classifies each
      INCLUDED (op-cert lane runs under it) or NO_FILTER (no `paths`/`branches` filter excludes an
      op-cert-bearing change); a test asserts the audit comment is present and lists every trigger.
